arts.ttl: Juventus coach Thiago Motta reveals Unai Emery's 'great bluff' and Aston Villa stance
arts.sum: Motta played under Emery at Paris Saint-Germain between 2016 and 2018 and will face his former manager for the first time at Villa Park. Juventus boss Thiago Motta doesn't believe Unai Emery would settle for a point on Wednesday night after the Aston Villa manager claimed a draw would be a good result for his side.

arts.ttl: Thiago Motta responds to Emery’s Aston-Villa Juventus claim: ‘I’m not bluffing’
arts.sum: Thiago Motta insisted that he is not ‘bluffing’ when it comes to the severity of...

arts.ttl: Thiago Motta: “Injuries aren’t coincidental, It’s happening to many clubs
arts.sum: Juventus manager Thiago Motta addressed the media ahead of their crucial Champions League match against Aston Villa in Birmingham. Despite a delayed start due to airport issues, Motta discussed various topics surrounding the team’s preparation and future. When asked about aiming for a draw, Motta questioned Emery’s sincerity, stating, “I’d like to see if Emery was serious or if he turned red when he spoke; for me, he’s lying. They’re a great team, they’ve earned 9 points in this Champions League, and we must be determined to do our job, giving our maximum to deliver a great performance.” Regarding the The post Thiago Motta: “Injuries aren’t coincidental, It’s happening to many clubs appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Matchday Guide: Aston Villa v Juventus
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Juventus - Wed, Nov 27th 2024, 20:00 Champions League action is back at Villa Park on Wednesday night as Aston Villa play host to Juventus. Having triumphed over Bayern Munich and Bologna on home soil, the Villans will be looking to continue their impressive record in B6 and get back to winning ways following defeat at Club Brugge on matchday four. Here's everything you need to know ahead of the encounter to enjoy your visit to Villa Park on matchday... Getting to the game Click on the sections below to learn more about tickets, Villa Park and transport to the stadium. Mobile Tickets Villa Park is now fully NFC ticketing enabled for supporters to access the stadium. Near-Field Communication (NFC) enables fans to enter the stadium via Apple or Google Wallet. Supporters can now also access their match-by-match tickets and enter the stadium directly via the Aston Villa app. Simply log into your Digital Account in the app and your tickets will appear under the ‘Tickets’ section. Supporters must update their app to the latest version to ensure in-app ticketing works without issue. Tickets A limited number of hospitality and GA+ tickets remain for the match. Book online here. The best way to secure tickets for future matches at Villa Park is via a Membership. Get Matchday Ready Ahead of the match at Villa Park, the club would like to advise supporters of the following ticketing information to ensure everyone enjoys their visit to our stadium. Fans will need to provide one of the two specified ticket types to gain entry to the stadium - access tickets in our official Aston Villa mobile app or download to Apple/Google Wallet for NFC entry. Hospitality and Premium Experiences The club has a range of new hospitality and GA+ areas to complement our existing offer. Check out all the information and book online. Hospitality bookings for our upcoming home matches can be made over the phone on 0333 323 1982 (option 1) or by emailing hospitality@avfc.co.uk. Under-3s Policy Many supporters may not be aware of the policy regarding under-3s, so we are reminding supporters that under-3’s are only permitted to be in the Family areas in the Trinity Road stand at Villa Park and must have a match ticket. Further information can be found here Aston Villa Ticketing (avfc.co.uk) Visiting Villa Park It's important that supporters take steps to plan their visit to the stadium. Turnstiles open at 6.30pm, so make sure you arrive at the stadium in plenty of time ahead of kick-off to ensure smooth entry. Please note, access to stiles is maintained during the first half and will close once the half-time whistle is blown. However you're planning on getting to the match, take a look at our Travel & Parking guide before leaving home. A traffic exclusion zone will be in operation around the stadium, meaning there will be less traffic on Trinity Road and Witton Lane from up to two hours before the match starts. The Holte Pub will open three hours prior to kick-off and close two hours after full-time. Parking and Travel Supporters are advised that the supply of car parking around Villa Park is limited. Fans can pre-book their space via either of the links below: www.astonvillaparking.co.uk www.countrywidemanagementsolutions.co.uk We will continue to work with local parking operators to offer fans the opportunity to book parking near the stadium. We would strongly encourage all supporters who have not pre-booked their car parking to travel to Villa Park by alternative modes. Covered cycle parking is available at our Inner City Academy on Witton Road. Plan ahead and check train timetables for direct services or travel earlier for less busy services. Visit Transport for West Midlands’ Journey Planner or the National Rail Enquiries real-time journey planner here. There are a number of Park and Ride sites across the region, for further details visit Park and Ride. To help you plan your journey by public transport, visit How to get to Villa Park. There is no planned disruption to key road, rail and bus routes to Villa Park on Saturday. Supporters are advised the city centre is likely to be busier than normal as the Christmas Market is now live. Please also be aware that the area behind the Trinity Road Stand is expected to be very busy ahead of the arrival of Aston Villa's team coach. City Centre and Tamworth Shuttle Buses Aston Villa is delighted to confirm to supporters that our AV82 city centre shuttle bus service will continue to run on matchdays this season. This follows a successful trial of the service at the back end of last season. The service will be in operation for all men’s home matches in the Premier League, UEFA Champions League and all domestic cup competitions, helping supporters travel more easily to and from Villa Park. Run by Stagecoach, the AV82 shuttle picks supporters up from outside Birmingham New Street Station on Dudley Street, running directly to Villa Park. Buses run every 15 minutes, starting three hours before kick-off for every home fixture. Services run back to Birmingham New Street at regular intervals for up to 90 minutes after the final whistle of every game, with services departing from Grosvenor Road, on the walking route towards Aston Station. The club also has a dedicated bus service, AV1, that runs to Villa Park from Tamworth on matchday. You can purchase tickets here. More information is available here. Accessibility information Supporters with accessibility requirements travelling to Villa Park by rail are advised to look out for the accessibility signage on both platforms at Witton Station. Station staff will be able to help you if you wait in the designated areas. More information is available here. For any supporters with additional mobility needs who have questions about travel to and from Villa Park on a matchday, please contact accessibility@avfc.co.uk. Pre-match activities Click on the sections below to learn more about activities taking place at Villa Park before kick-off. Player arrivals Aston Villa would like to advise supporters of a change to the first team arrival protocol for our next two UEFA Champions League home fixtures with Juventus and Celtic. For these two matches, the team will now arrive via Trinity Road and the Trinity Road Stand reception area, as was the case towards the end of last season. This decision has been taken following consultation with West Midlands Police and security advice, with away supporters congregating along Witton Lane earlier than would be usual for Premier League fixtures. Away team arrivals will remain the same and the process of arrival via the Fan Zone in the North Stand car park will remain in place for Premier League matches. For more information, click here. Enjoy the Fan Zone Villa Park's Fan Zone is again open on Wednesday, located in the former Players' Car Park. The new space opens its doors at 5pm, with supporters able to enjoy a bar area, food and big screen TVs. We also have entertainment taking place on the stage opposite Trinity Reception, including live music from Conor Michael. Please note, Aston Villa's team welcome will take place via the Trinity Road Stand reception at approximately 6.30pm. Juventus' team arrival will remain the same via the Fan Zone in the North Stand car park. For more information, click here. We want to see your experience of matchday at Villa Park! Post your photos and tag #UTV and you could be featured on the big screens at the stadium! Family Fun Zone Work is soon to begin on the transformation of the existing Academy building at Villa Park. Unfortunately, due to the planned works, the Academy building is not operational to the extent to which we can safely open the Family Fun Zone. The club are working on alternate arrangements to enable a continuation of some of the family-friendly pre-match activities. Updates will follow in the coming weeks. Food and Beverage Villa Park offers a wide range of cuisine and beverages on a matchday. Outside the stadium, several street food trucks will sit alongside a bar serving alcoholic and soft drinks from 5pm. Cashless Stadium Please note, Villa Park will be cashless to help ensure safe social distancing practices and minimise contact at all points of sale. This includes the Villa Store, with card payments widely accepted in and around the stadium. The only exception is programme kiosks outside the ground, which will still accept cash. In-stadia betting kiosks will also be in operation for this fixture and such service will be provided on a cashless basis. The Warehouse Design Update The Warehouse, which will now be 40,000 sqft, has been further developed with an enhanced design to accommodate live music and entertainment events for 3,500+ visitors and fans. Structural alterations to over half the rooftop will make way for a newly raised flat roof which will accommodate a generous mezzanine floor and viewing galley from above for both live music and fan zone entertainment. Creating a multi-use space called ‘The Warehouse’, will include a diverse range of Food & Beverage offers and pre- and post-match and event entertainment. The Warehouse will become a vibrant and ‘go to’ destination for the community embedded in the Villa Park and Witton Street scene. Matchday Programme Supporters can order a copy of the official matchday programme for Wednesday night's match against the Italian giants, which is packed with features and exclusive content. Copies of the Aston Villa News & Record will be available to purchase on matchday from sellers around the ground and inside the Villa Store, which will have programmes available from Tuesday. If you'd like to secure a copy, supporters can order online here for home delivery. Villa Store Click on the sections below to learn about merchandise points around the stadium. Store Opening Hours There are a number of places where supporters can shop for Aston Villa merchandise on a matchday, including the upgraded Villa Store. Below you'll find the opening times for each shop around the ground... Villa Store: 9am-10.30pm Holte Store: 5-8pm and 9.30-10.30pm 82 Lounge: 5-8pm and 8.30-10.30pm Lower Grounds: 5-8pm and 8.30-10.30pm Trinity Store: 6.30pm until end of half-time Black Friday Black Friday is almost here and you can be the first to hear about our exciting deals coming up. From Wednesday, November 27, you will be able to shop Villa merchandise online and in the Villa Store, snap up Hospitality & GA+ tickets, memberships and much more in our biggest Black Friday sale yet. Register for a reminder to be the first to receive some of our best deals yet - make sure you don't miss out! During the game Click on the sections below to learn more about events that take place during the match. See it...hear it...report it! If you witness discriminatory behaviour at a match or around a stadium, please report it to the police or a steward. Supporters at Villa Park can text REPORT to 60060 along with the location of the incident and details of what you have witnessed. The text line also includes the reporting of any safeguarding concerns. The text service will be supported by our security team and West Midlands Police. Each text costs one standard message at your network rate. The club may share your details with West Midlands Police if further information is required to assist with a criminal prosecution. All-seater Stadium We would like to remind supporters that Villa Park is an all-seater stadium. In all areas of Villa Park, we have children, elderly fans and those with medical conditions who are unable to see or watch the match if supporters stand up in front of them. Please be mindful of this at all times and thank you for your cooperation. Fan Engagement Click the sections below to learn about how you can get involved on matchday and our fan loyalty programme, Villa Rewards. Get Involved New for 2024, the ‘Villa Predictor’ game will give fans the opportunity to win a £500 store voucher if they correctly predict the outcome of the game. T&Cs apply. We want to know about your matchday experience at Villa Park. Tweet us using #UTV and you could feature on the big screens at the stadium! Pride Rewards The Pride Rewards Portal is back up and running. Log in here. AV150 Legacy Wall Aston Villa is excited to reveal a new AV150 Legacy Wall at Villa Park as part of our 150th anniversary celebrations. Supporters have the opportunity to become part of our iconic home, wherever you are in the world, by purchasing your very own personalised plaque at Villa Park. Our Legacy Wall is a place where you, your friends and family can show your support for the club, celebrate births, marriages, anniversaries or pay tribute to loved ones. For more information, click here.

arts.ttl: Juventus in talks with Benfica for Antonio Silva
arts.sum: Juventus have set their sights on Antonio Silva, a promising young defender from Benfica, as they look to strengthen their squad in the upcoming January transfer window. According to Tuttosport, negotiations between the two clubs are already underway. Silva, a 21-year-old Portuguese international with 17 caps, has caught the attention of several top European clubs due to his impressive performances. Benfica values Silva at a minimum of €40 million, reflecting his potential and current standing in European football. Juventus see Silva as a key target to bolster their defensive line, particularly as they plan for the future. The club’s sporting The post Juventus in talks with Benfica for Antonio Silva appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Chelsea, Aston Villa and Manchester City men all pulled into claim of ‘complete ridicule’ for national boss
arts.sum: Domenico Tedesco is facing a fight to remain as Belgium manager and if the treatment he’s received from players at Chelsea, Manchester City and Aston Villa is anything to go by, it’s a fight he won’t win. That’s at least according to Het Laatste Nieuws, via Voetbal 24, who cover the likes of Romeo Lavia, The post Chelsea, Aston Villa and Manchester City men all pulled into claim of ‘complete ridicule’ for national boss appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: I've spoken to Luiz after Villa transfer - it's a difficult moment for him
arts.sum: "Now with Douglas Luiz playing there, I know he’s in a difficult moment but he’s a really good player. Pau Torres has revealed he's spoken with Douglas Luiz after the midfielder's struggles at Juventus following his summer transfer from Aston Villa.
